packTitle: Picnic Point Parking Structure Feasibility — Ground Level + 2-Floor with Overpass Integration

modules.title: Site Survey & Geotechnical Investigation: LiDAR Topographic Survey, Subsurface Borings SPT/CPT, Slope Stability Analysis, Foundation System Recommendation, Seismic Design Category D, Glacial Till Bearing Capacity

Structural Engineering & Design: Open-Air Precast Concrete Parking Structure, IBC 2021 Seismic Zone D, ASCE 7-22 Load Calculations, Level 3 Deck-to-Overpass Tie-In Bridge, ADA Accessible Vertical Circulation, 30% Design Drawings

Permitting & Regulatory Pathway: Snohomish County PDS Building Permit, SEPA Environmental Checklist, Shoreline Substantial Development Permit, Critical Areas Review, BNSF Railway Coordination, Land Disturbing Activity Permit

Stormwater & Environmental Engineering: Impervious Surface Calculation, LID BMP Selection, Snohomish County Drainage Manual MR 1-9, Oil/Water Separator, Bioretention Planters, Permeable Pavement, NPDES Phase I Compliance, Shellfish Closure Nexus

Cost Estimating & Funding Strategy: RSMeans Pacific Northwest Calibration, Precast Parking Structure $80-$100/sqft, Per-Stall Cost $28K-$35K, Two-Phase Construction, RCO Grants, FHWA Transportation Alternatives, WA Wildlife and Recreation Program, 20-Year Life-Cycle Comparison

bibliography: Snohomish County PDS — Structural Plans Requirements, Critical Area Requirements, SEPA

U.S. Access Board — PROWAG Final Rule August 2023
Washington State Dept of Ecology — SEPA, Shoreline Permitting Manual, NPDES Phase I
Snohomish County Parks — Lord Hill Park Parking Improvements (OTAK 2024), PRVP 2022
Clark Pacific — Precast + Parking, PARC System, PHMF
Schaefer Inc — Parking Structure Options: Precast vs Cast-in-Place
Kiwi Newton — How Much Does It Cost to Build a Parking Structure?
safetyRules: SC-GEO: All bearing capacity, slope stability, settlement claims must come from licensed WA geotechnical engineer — no values assumed without site-specific investigation (ABSOLUTE)
SC-BNSF: Any overpass modification statement requires BNSF coordination — do not represent as feasible without railroad review (GATE)
SC-SEPA: Present MDNS vs DS pathways as options — do not predict which the county will issue (ANNOTATE)
SC-SHELL: Present stormwater improvement as potential contributing factor — do not claim it will resolve shellfish closure (ANNOTATE)
SC-COST: All cost figures are Class 4 pre-design estimates with ±50% variance — do not present as bids (GATE)
SC-ADA: Present ADA requirements per US Access Board and FHWA — do not assert compliance without engineer of record review (GATE)
SC-TULALIP: Ground disturbance in Snohomish County triggers Unanticipated Discovery obligations — Tulalip Tribes must be notified as part of SEPA (ABSOLUTE)
